evals.prompt: I need to set up a new Playwright project from scratch. Create a complete project structure with proper folder organization, naming conventions, and a working playwright.config.ts. Use the recommended folder structure with tests/, pages/, fixtures/, and test-data/ directories. Include example files to show the pattern.
evals.expected_output: Complete project structure with playwright.config.ts, sample Page Object, sample test file, fixtures, and proper folder hierarchy

assertions.text: tests/ directory with at least one .spec.ts file
pages/ directory with Page Object using Page suffix
Role-based or label-based selectors used (getByRole, getByLabel)

evals.prompt: Write E2E tests for a login flow using Playwright's test() and expect(). The app has email/password fields, a submit button, and shows error messages for invalid credentials. Create the LoginPage Page Object and tests for: successful login, wrong password, and empty fields validation.
evals.expected_output: LoginPage.ts with locators and methods, login.spec.ts with test cases for success, failure, and validation

assertions.text: At least 3 test cases: successful login, wrong password, empty fields
LoginPage uses getByLabel or getByRole selectors
No assertions inside Page Object (no expect() in LoginPage.ts)
Tests are independent using beforeEach or per-test setup

evals.prompt: Debug this flaky test that fails intermittently - it tries to click a button that appears after a loading spinner, but sometimes the click happens before the button is clickable. Fix the test to make it stable. Here's the current code: import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'; test('submit form', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('/form'); await page.click('#submit-btn'); await expect(page.locator('.success')).toBeVisible(); });
evals.expected_output: Fixed test code with proper waiting strategy and comments explaining why it was flaky

assertions.text: Explanation of why original test was flaky
Fix waits for spinner hidden or button enabled before clicking
Role-based selector preferred over CSS ID (#submit-btn)

evals.prompt: Write an API test for a POST /api/login endpoint. The request body is {email, password} and response is {token, user: {id, name, email}}. I need to validate: HTTP 200 for valid credentials, HTTP 401 for invalid, response time under 500ms, and JSON schema matches expected structure.
evals.expected_output: API test file with status code assertions, schema validation, response time check, and error case handling

assertions.text: HTTP status asserted before response body in all tests
JSON schema covers token and user fields
